title: Job Interview
description: Key vocabulary and phrases to succeed in English job interviews

video.title: Job Interview English: Common Questions & Answers

vocabulary.meaning: A document summarizing education, work experience and skills
vocabulary.example: Please email your resume to our HR department.

vocabulary.meaning: A skill or achievement that makes you suitable for a job
vocabulary.example: Do you have the qualifications required for this role?

vocabulary.meaning: A letter of introduction sent with a resume
vocabulary.example: Your cover letter should explain why you want this job.

vocabulary.meaning: A person applying for a job or position
vocabulary.example: We interviewed ten candidates for the position.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who finds and hires employees
vocabulary.example: A recruiter reached out to me on LinkedIn.

vocabulary.meaning: A recruiter who seeks talent for specific senior roles
vocabulary.example: A headhunter approached me with a great offer.

vocabulary.meaning: A skill or ability required to perform a job well
vocabulary.example: Leadership is a key competency for this role.

vocabulary.meaning: An interview question about past experience using STAR method
vocabulary.example: Tell me about a time you handled conflict ‚ÄĒ that's a behavioral question.

vocabulary.meaning: Situation, Task, Action, Result ‚ÄĒ a framework for answering interview questions
vocabulary.example: Use the STAR method to structure your answers.

vocabulary.meaning: A trial period at the start of a job to assess performance
vocabulary.example: The probationary period is three months.

vocabulary.meaning: Total pay including salary, bonuses and benefits
vocabulary.example: What compensation package does the role offer?

vocabulary.meaning: Non-wage perks like insurance, leave and allowances
vocabulary.example: The company offers great health benefits.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who can vouch for your abilities and character
vocabulary.example: Please provide two professional references.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of integrating a new employee into a company
vocabulary.example: Onboarding will take one week.

vocabulary.meaning: Natural ability to learn or do something
vocabulary.example: He showed a strong aptitude for problem-solving.

vocabulary.meaning: The ability to take action without being told
vocabulary.example: We value candidates who show initiative.

vocabulary.meaning: The ability to adjust to new conditions
vocabulary.example: Adaptability is crucial in a fast-changing industry.

vocabulary.meaning: Abilities to communicate and interact with others effectively
vocabulary.example: Strong interpersonal skills are essential in sales.

vocabulary.meaning: The ability to analyze and evaluate information objectively
vocabulary.example: Critical thinking is tested in our assessment.

vocabulary.meaning: A belief in the value and importance of working hard
vocabulary.example: She is known for her strong work ethic.

vocabulary.meaning: Skills and qualities you are particularly good at
vocabulary.example: What are your main strengths for this position?

vocabulary.meaning: Areas where you need improvement
vocabulary.example: Be honest when discussing your weaknesses.

vocabulary.meaning: The sequence of jobs leading toward a career goal
vocabulary.example: Where do you see your career path in five years?

vocabulary.meaning: The ability to guide and inspire a team
vocabulary.example: Tell me about your leadership experience.

vocabulary.meaning: Working cooperatively with others to achieve a goal
vocabulary.example: Teamwork is a core value of our culture.

vocabulary.meaning: Handling multiple tasks at the same time
vocabulary.example: Can you give an example of effective multitasking?

vocabulary.meaning: A time by which something must be completed
vocabulary.example: How do you manage tasks when facing tight deadlines?

vocabulary.meaning: The time between resignation and last working day
vocabulary.translation: thŠĽĚi gian b√°o tr∆įŠĽõc khi nghŠĽČ
vocabulary.example: My notice period is one month.

vocabulary.meaning: The salary you hope to receive in a new role
vocabulary.example: What are your salary expectations?

vocabulary.meaning: Working from a location outside the main office
vocabulary.example: Is remote work available for this position?

vocabulary.meaning: Chances to develop skills and advance in a career
vocabulary.example: I'm looking for growth opportunities in my next role.

vocabulary.meaning: An interview conducted by multiple interviewers at once
vocabulary.example: You'll face a panel interview with three managers.

vocabulary.meaning: How well a candidate matches a company's values and environment
vocabulary.translation: ph√Ļ hŠĽ£p vńÉn h√≥a c√īng ty
vocabulary.example: Cultural fit matters as much as technical skills here.

vocabulary.meaning: A question requiring a detailed answer, not just yes or no
vocabulary.example: Tell me about yourself is an open-ended question.

vocabulary.meaning: Something achieved successfully
vocabulary.example: Share your greatest professional accomplishment.

vocabulary.meaning: Intense enjoyment and eagerness
vocabulary.example: Show enthusiasm throughout the interview.

vocabulary.meaning: To express ideas clearly and effectively
vocabulary.example: She was able to articulate her goals very clearly.

vocabulary.meaning: To discuss terms to reach a mutual agreement
vocabulary.example: Don't be afraid to negotiate your salary offer.

vocabulary.meaning: A collection of work samples showing your abilities
vocabulary.example: Please bring your portfolio to the interview.

vocabulary.meaning: Verification of a candidate's history and credentials
vocabulary.example: We conduct background checks on all new hires.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal offer of employment from a company
vocabulary.example: I received a job offer with a great package.

vocabulary.meaning: Being turned down for a position applied to
vocabulary.example: Don't be discouraged by rejection ‚ÄĒ keep applying.

vocabulary.meaning: Non-technical interpersonal skills like communication and empathy
vocabulary.example: Soft skills are just as important as technical skills.

vocabulary.meaning: Specific technical abilities learned through training
vocabulary.example: Python and SQL are hard skills for this role.

vocabulary.meaning: The initial opinion formed about someone
vocabulary.example: A firm handshake creates a good first impression.

vocabulary.meaning: Rules about what is appropriate to wear
vocabulary.example: Business formal is the dress code for the interview.

vocabulary.meaning: To make contact after an initial meeting or interview
vocabulary.example: Always follow up with a thank-you email after an interview.

vocabulary.meaning: A reduced list of the best candidates selected for the next stage
vocabulary.example: You've been shortlisted for the second interview.

vocabulary.meaning: The person responsible for making the hiring decision
vocabulary.example: The hiring manager will call you this afternoon.

vocabulary.meaning: A trial period at the start of employment
vocabulary.example: She passed her three-month probation successfully.

vocabulary.meaning: A brief persuasive speech to introduce yourself or an idea
vocabulary.translation: giŠĽõi thiŠĽáu bŠļ£n th√Ęn ngŠļĮn gŠĽćn
vocabulary.example: Prepare a 30-second elevator pitch about your strengths.

vocabulary.meaning: Your long-term professional aspirations
vocabulary.example: Interviewers often ask about your career goals.

vocabulary.meaning: An interview focusing on past behaviour to predict future performance
vocabulary.example: A behavioural interview uses the STAR method.

vocabulary.meaning: Structured around demonstrated skills and abilities
vocabulary.example: This is a competency-based interview with five questions.

vocabulary.meaning: An assessment measuring personality, aptitude or intelligence
vocabulary.example: Candidates must complete a psychometric test before the interview.

vocabulary.meaning: An exam measuring natural ability for a particular task
vocabulary.example: Pass the aptitude test to advance to the interview stage.

vocabulary.meaning: A multi-activity evaluation event for job candidates
vocabulary.translation: trung t√Ęm ńĎ√°nh gi√° ŠĽ©ng vi√™n
vocabulary.example: The final stage is a full-day assessment centre.

vocabulary.meaning: A team activity used to assess how candidates work with others
vocabulary.example: The group exercise tests collaboration skills.

vocabulary.meaning: An interview where you solve a real business problem
vocabulary.example: Consulting firms often use a case study interview.

vocabulary.meaning: A senior-level interview with company leadership
vocabulary.translation: phŠĽŹng vŠļ•n vŠĽõi ban l√£nh ńĎŠļ°o
vocabulary.example: The final step is an executive interview with the CEO.

vocabulary.meaning: An interview testing specific technical knowledge or skills
vocabulary.example: Software engineers typically go through a technical interview.

vocabulary.meaning: A legal agreement between employer and employee
vocabulary.example: Read the employment contract carefully before signing.

vocabulary.meaning: The full compensation including salary and benefits
vocabulary.example: The package includes a base salary, bonus and health insurance.

vocabulary.meaning: Fixed regular pay before bonuses or extras
vocabulary.example: The base salary is $50,000 per year.

vocabulary.meaning: Extra payment given for good performance
vocabulary.example: She received a performance bonus at year end.

vocabulary.meaning: Pay based on the amount of sales made
vocabulary.example: Sales staff earn a commission on every deal.

vocabulary.meaning: Paid vacation time given by an employer per year
vocabulary.example: The role offers 20 days of annual leave.

vocabulary.meaning: A flexible model combining remote and in-office work
vocabulary.example: The company offers hybrid work ‚ÄĒ three days in the office.

vocabulary.meaning: A healthy division between professional and personal time
vocabulary.translation: c√Ęn bŠļĪng c√īng viŠĽác v√† cuŠĽôc sŠĽĎng
vocabulary.example: I asked about work-life balance during the interview.

vocabulary.meaning: Opportunities to grow skills and advance in a career
vocabulary.example: Strong career development prospects attracted me to this role.

vocabulary.meaning: Guidance provided by an experienced person to a less experienced one
vocabulary.example: The company offers a structured mentorship programme.

vocabulary.meaning: An advertisement for a vacant position
vocabulary.example: I found the job posting on LinkedIn.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal summary of the duties and requirements of a role
vocabulary.example: Read the job description carefully before applying.

vocabulary.meaning: Positive qualities or skills that make you a good candidate
vocabulary.example: List your key strengths in your cover letter.

vocabulary.meaning: Areas where you have room for improvement
vocabulary.example: Be honest but strategic when discussing your weaknesses.

vocabulary.meaning: Skills applicable across different jobs and industries
vocabulary.translation: kŠĽĻ nńÉng c√≥ thŠĽÉ chuyŠĽÉn ńĎŠĽēi
vocabulary.example: Communication is one of the most transferable skills.

vocabulary.meaning: Moving from one type of job or industry to a different one
vocabulary.example: She made a career change from law to marketing.

vocabulary.meaning: Building professional relationships to help your career
vocabulary.example: Networking on LinkedIn can open many job opportunities.

vocabulary.meaning: A professional social networking platform used for jobs
vocabulary.translation: mŠļ°ng x√£ hŠĽôi nghŠĽĀ nghiŠĽáp LinkedIn
vocabulary.example: Update your LinkedIn profile before applying.

vocabulary.meaning: A recruiter who actively seeks candidates for senior positions
vocabulary.example: A headhunter approached her about a director role.

vocabulary.meaning: People who can speak about your work performance
vocabulary.translation: ng∆įŠĽĚi tham chiŠļŅu, ng∆įŠĽĚi giŠĽõi thiŠĽáu
vocabulary.example: Provide two professional references when asked.

vocabulary.meaning: Moving to a new place for a job
vocabulary.example: The job requires relocation to Singapore.

vocabulary.meaning: Working from outside the traditional office, often from home
vocabulary.example: More companies now offer remote work options.

vocabulary.meaning: Comments provided about a candidate's interview performance
vocabulary.example: Always ask for interview feedback if you are unsuccessful.

vocabulary.meaning: The drive to outperform others
vocabulary.example: Your competitiveness will help you succeed in a sales role.

vocabulary.meaning: A temporary stop in hiring new employees
vocabulary.example: The company implemented a headcount freeze during the recession.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal letter informing an employer you are leaving
vocabulary.example: She handed in her resignation letter with two weeks notice.

vocabulary.meaning: A meeting with HR when leaving a company
vocabulary.example: The exit interview revealed common reasons people were leaving.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of finding and hiring skilled employees
vocabulary.example: The talent acquisition team uses LinkedIn and job fairs.

vocabulary.meaning: Moving to a different role within the same organization
vocabulary.translation: di chuyŠĽÉn nŠĽôi bŠĽô trong c√īng ty
vocabulary.example: The company encourages internal mobility for career growth.

vocabulary.meaning: A temporary position, often for students, to gain work experience
vocabulary.example: She completed a six-month internship at a tech firm.

vocabulary.meaning: Working independently on a contract basis rather than as an employee
vocabulary.example: He does freelance graphic design for several companies.

vocabulary.meaning: Software that manages job applications and candidate data
vocabulary.translation: hŠĽá thŠĽĎng quŠļ£n l√Ĺ ŠĽ©ng tuyŠĽÉn (ATS)
vocabulary.example: Use keywords in your CV to pass the applicant tracking system.

vocabulary.meaning: Support provided by a company to help laid-off employees find new work
vocabulary.example: The company offered outplacement services after the layoffs.

vocabulary.meaning: A period when a person was not employed
vocabulary.translation: khoŠļ£ng thŠĽĚi gian kh√īng c√≥ viŠĽác l√†m
vocabulary.example: Be prepared to explain any employment gap on your CV.

vocabulary.meaning: An offer made in response to an initial job offer to negotiate better terms
vocabulary.translation: ńĎŠĽĀ nghŠĽč phŠļ£n hŠĽďi / trŠļ£ gi√° l∆į∆°ng
vocabulary.example: She made a counter offer asking for 10% more salary.

vocabulary.meaning: An event where employers and job seekers meet
vocabulary.example: Hundreds of students attended the university job fair.

vocabulary.meaning: Legal authorization to work in a particular country
vocabulary.translation: quyŠĽĀn ńĎ∆įŠĽ£c l√†m viŠĽác hŠĽ£p ph√°p
vocabulary.example: Employers must verify your right to work in this country.

vocabulary.meaning: An official document allowing a foreign national to work in a country
vocabulary.example: She applied for a work permit before accepting the job.

vocabulary.meaning: An investigation into a candidate's past employment and criminal record
vocabulary.example: All staff undergo a background check before starting.

quiz.question: What is a 'cover letter'?
options: A letter of introduction sent with a resume

quiz.question: What does the STAR method stand for?
What is a 'probationary period'?
options: A trial period to assess performance

quiz.question: What is a 'panel interview'?
options: An interview on a TV panel
An interview with multiple interviewers at once

quiz.question: What is a 'notice period'?
options: Time between resignation and last working day
Time to study for a job

quiz.question: What does it mean to 'negotiate' your salary?
options: Discuss terms to reach a mutual agreement

quiz.question: What is 'cultural fit'?
options: How well a candidate matches the company's values

quiz.question: What is a 'shortlist'?
options: A reduced list of the best candidates

quiz.question: Why should you send a follow-up after an interview?
options: To show gratitude and maintain a good impression
To ask for a higher salary immediately

quiz.question: What is an 'assessment centre'?
options: A multi-activity evaluation event for candidates

quiz.question: What is a 'competency-based interview'?
options: An interview structured around demonstrated skills

quiz.question: What is an 'internship'?
options: A temporary position to gain work experience

quiz.question: What does 'networking' mean in a career context?
options: Building professional relationships to help your career
Applying to many jobs at once
